diagrams.title: Basic Union-Find Structure
diagrams.description: Shows a collection of elements with parent pointers forming a forest of trees. Each node points to its parent, with root nodes pointing to themselves. This visualizes how Union-Find represents disjoint sets.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental data structure representation where each element belongs to a set represented by a tree rooted at the set representative

diagrams.title: Union by Rank Operation
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the Union operation where two sets are merged. Shows how the shorter tree (by height/rank) attaches under the root of the taller tree to keep the structure balanced.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the Union by Rank optimization that keeps the tree height logarithmic, ensuring efficient Find operations

diagrams.title: Path Compression during Find
diagrams.description: Shows the Find operation where along the path to the root, each node's parent pointer is directly updated to point to the root. This flattens the tree for future operations.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the Path Compression optimization that makes subsequent Find operations nearly O(1) by flattening the tree structure
